name: OpenClaw Accessibility
description: Registers the a11y_audit tool: runs axe-core against a page or raw HTML over a CDP-reachable Chromium (local or remote/managed, e.g. AWS Bedrock AgentCore Browser) and returns structured WCAG findings { ok, standard, target, summary, violations }, failing open to { ok:false, error } so a bad audit can never break the agent turn. Ships two skills (accessibility, a11y-auditor) that drive the tool.

browserProvider.description: How the browser is supplied. 'mcp' (default) audits through the agent's existing browser MCP tools (no new connection). 'cdp' attaches to a standing endpoint (cdpEndpoint/cdpHeaders). 'agentcore' starts a short-lived AWS Bedrock AgentCore browser session per audit.

mcp.description: Config for browserProvider: 'mcp'. Audits via api.runtime.callTool against the agent's existing browser MCP tools — reusing the open session, no new connection.

serverName.description: MCP server name the browser tools are registered under (runtime-specific).

waitUntil.description: Page navigation wait condition. Default 'load'. For SPA / managed browsers, 'networkidle' or 'domcontentloaded' avoids auditing a half-rendered page.

agentcore.description: Config for browserProvider: 'agentcore'. IAM auth comes from the agent's ambient AWS credentials — no keys here.

region.description: AWS region of the AgentCore browser.

identifier.description: Browser tool identifier to start a session against. Defaults to aws.browser.v1.

sessionTimeoutSeconds.description: Upper-bound TTL for the per-audit session (started and stopped each audit).

cdpEndpoint.description: CDP endpoint to attach to when browserProvider is 'cdp'. http(s):// for a local browser, or ws(s):// for a remote/managed browser. Set per agent at deploy.

cdpHeaders.description: Optional HTTP headers sent on the CDP connect handshake. Use for a browser that authenticates the CDP socket (e.g. AgentCore signed Authorization headers). Omit for a local browser.

connectTimeoutMs.description: Timeout for the CDP connect handshake.

defaultStandard.description: axe standard used when a call omits `standard`.

timeoutMs.description: Max time for one audit before failing open with `timeout`.
